extractions.extraction_text: I value the drawing at $233.95 so trust that this settles the matter.

attributes.mechanism: The author unilaterally assigns an arbitrary value to a drawing and presents its acceptance as a foregone conclusion, thereby shifting the burden of disputing this 'settlement' onto the recipient.

extractions.extraction_text: Can I have my drawing of a spider back then please?

attributes.mechanism: The author recontextualizes the 'payment' drawing as their personal property, forcing the recipient to engage with the absurd premise that a payment can be reclaimed.

extractions.extraction_text: Are you sure this drawing of a spider is the one I sent you?

attributes.mechanism: The author casts doubt on the authenticity of the drawing the recipient possesses, reframing it as potentially not the original 'payment' and creating a new problem for the recipient to resolve.

extractions.extraction_text: This spider only has seven legs and I do not feel I would have made such an elementary mistake when I drew it.

attributes.mechanism: The author applies a formal, critical tone and detailed observation to a trivial error in a spider drawing, elevating a petty detail to the level of a significant factual discrepancy.

extractions.extraction_text: Thank you for contacting me.

attributes.mechanism: The author uses a standard professional courtesy in a context where they are actively creating problems, making the politeness feel disingenuous and impossible to challenge.

extractions.extraction_text: I am currently away on leave, traveling through time, and will be returning last week.

attributes.mechanism: The author introduces a plausible reason for absence and then compounds it with increasingly impossible details about time travel, presenting each step as a natural and unremarkable continuation.

extractions.extraction_text: I realise, with hindsight, that it is possible you rejected the drawing of the spider due to this obvious limb omission but did not point it out in an effort to avoid hurting my feelings.

attributes.mechanism: The author reinterprets the recipient's potential rejection of the drawing as an act of kindness to spare their feelings, thereby validating the absurd premise of the 'limb omission' and shifting the burden of explanation.

extractions.extraction_text: As such, I am sending you a revised drawing with the correct number of legs as full payment for any amount outstanding.

attributes.mechanism: The author unilaterally declares a new, equally absurd drawing as 'full payment' for an outstanding amount, thereby shifting the burden of accepting or disputing this new, unsolicited 'payment' onto the recipient.

extractions.extraction_text: Jane, I understand and will certainly make a payment this week if I remember.

attributes.mechanism: The author performs understanding and a commitment to payment using polite language, but immediately undermines it with a casual 'if I remember,' rendering the sincerity hollow and the commitment unreliable.

extractions.extraction_text: It was silly to assume I could provide you with something of no value, waste your time, and attach such a large amount to it. As you have not accepted my second drawing as payment, please return the drawing to me as soon as possible.

attributes.mechanism: The author feigns an admission of their own 'silly' assumption, then leverages the recipient's entirely reasonable non-acceptance to impose a new obligation, shifting the burden of returning the drawing onto the recipient while maintaining a facade of reasonableness.

extractions.extraction_text: I'm surprised you managed to attend work at all what with thinking about Missy out there cold, frightened and alone... possibly lying on the side of the road, her back legs squashed by a vehicle, calling out "Shannon, where are you?"

attributes.mechanism: The author introduces the plausible premise of the recipient's concern for a lost pet and then compounds it with increasingly graphic and anthropomorphic details, each presented as a natural continuation to heighten the recipient's guilt.

extractions.extraction_text: Although I have two clients expecting completed work this afternoon, I will, of course, drop everything and do whatever it takes to facilitate the speedy return of Missy.

attributes.mechanism: Performs extreme professional sacrifice for a trivial cause using corporate language, making the insincerity impossible to challenge without the challenger appearing unreasonable.

extractions.extraction_text: I never said I dislike cats.

attributes.mechanism: The author denies an implied accusation from the recipient, setting the stage to reframe their past actions or statements regarding cats on their own terms.

extractions.extraction_text: Steven sat down in a chair already occupied by a cat. The surprised cat clawed and snarled causing Steven to leap out of the chair, slip on a rug and strike his forehead onto the corner of a floor speaker. In its shock, the cat also defecated, leaving Steven with a wet brown stain down the back of his beige cargo pants.

attributes.mechanism: Starts with a mundane interaction and escalates with increasingly specific and disturbing details of injury and humiliation, each presented as a natural continuation of the last.

extractions.extraction_text: I liked that cat.

attributes.mechanism: Following a detailed and disturbing account of Steven's injury and humiliation, this brief emotionally vacant statement reveals the author's true satisfaction with the outcome through total flatness.

extractions.extraction_text: It's a design thing. The cat is lost in the negative space.

attributes.mechanism: Applies formal artistic language to rationalise the cat's violent and messy actions, reframing a harmful incident as a matter of aesthetic interpretation.

extractions.extraction_text: I don't come downstairs and tell you how to send text messages, log onto Facebook, and look out the window.

attributes.mechanism: Defensively shifts the burden of perceived interference onto the target by implying they are overstepping, while positioning the author as reasonable for not dictating trivial actions.

extractions.extraction_text: I brought in a box of donuts once, which was pretty nice.

attributes.mechanism: Describes a malicious act as 'pretty nice,' performing a social nicety while the actual intent is the opposite, making the insincerity difficult to challenge.

extractions.extraction_text: When he asked, "Who brought in these donuts?" I told him, "The girl from the shop across the road, because they have too many."

attributes.mechanism: Reframes the origin of trash donuts with a false plausible explanation, forcing the target to either accept the lie by consuming them or challenge a seemingly innocent statement.

extractions.extraction_text: There have actually been twelve formal complaints against me but two of those were complaining that nothing had been done about the previous formal complaints so I didn't bother scanning those in.

attributes.mechanism: Introduces the premise of formal complaints, then compounds it with the specific detail that some complaints were about unaddressed previous complaints, leading to the casual illogical conclusion of not processing them.

extractions.extraction_text: I agree that banning him from access to the computers at school is an appropriate punishment. Especially considering his enthusiasm for the subject.

attributes.mechanism: Endorses a counterproductive punishment with flat sincerity, then adds a single clause that reveals the author's contempt for the logic behind it without ever stating that contempt.

extractions.extraction_text: Once, when I coloured outside the lines, I was forced to stand in the playground with a sign around my neck that read 'non-conformist' while the other children pelted me with rubble from the recently torched school library.

attributes.mechanism: Begins with a trivial childhood misstep and progressively introduces increasingly specific, disturbing, and impossible details, each presented as a natural and logical consequence.

extractions.extraction_text: Also, if you happen to see Seb eating anything over the next few weeks, please remove the food from him immediately. He forgot to feed his turtle last night and I feel a month without food will help him understand both the importance of being a responsible pet owner and the effects of malnutrition.

attributes.mechanism: Imposes a severe punishment on a child for a minor transgression and delegates its enforcement to others, framing cruelty as a reasonable and educational consequence.

extractions.extraction_text: Especially during feeding time when I release live rabbits. Currently only eight but one is expecting. I'm hoping for a litter of ten as this is the number required for dog sled racing.

attributes.mechanism: Casually reveals predatory feeding practice, then escalates with specific animal counts, a pregnancy, and the absurd goal of dog sled racing in an apartment, all delivered as unremarkable facts.

extractions.extraction_text: No, the ducks in the bathroom aren't mine. They're very small ducks. The ducks will be flying south for the winter soon so it won't be an issue.

attributes.mechanism: Introduces a completely new bizarre element unprompted, then offers a series of increasingly implausible and irrelevant justifications, each presented as a reasonable resolution.

extractions.extraction_text: While I have no idea what a foggot is, I will assume it is a term of endearment

attributes.mechanism: Reframes a likely derogatory term as a compliment by feigning ignorance, forcing the recipient to either accept the absurd reinterpretation or engage in an awkward correction that confirms their own hostility.

extractions.extraction_text: and I appreciate you taking time out from calculating launch trajectories or removing temporal lobe tumors to contact me with such.

attributes.mechanism: Uses exaggerated praise for the recipient's supposed importance, performing polite appreciation that is clearly sarcastic and designed to diminish the insult by treating it as trivial.

extractions.extraction_text: I've attached a signed photo as per your request.

attributes.mechanism: Reframes a hostile email as an implicit request for a signed photo, converting the sender's aggression into evidence of admiration without their consent.

extractions.extraction_text: I would be delighted to spend my free time creating logos and pie charts for you based on further vague promises of future possible payment.

attributes.mechanism: Expresses delight at the prospect of working for free on vague promises, performing extreme generosity while the preceding context of unpaid work makes the sentiment clearly sarcastic.

extractions.extraction_text: Your last project was actually both commercially viable and original. Unfortunately the part that was commercially viable was not original, and the part that was original was not commercially viable.

attributes.mechanism: Reframes the target's implied claim of a successful project by splitting its positive attributes and negating them, forcing the target to engage with a definition that invalidates their original premise.

extractions.extraction_text: Last night, with my curtains closed and bedside light off, I read a book. Wearing sunglasses. Under a blanket.

attributes.mechanism: Introduces the mundane act of reading at night and escalates with increasingly specific and impossible protective measures, each delivered as a natural continuation implying an extreme unstated problem.

extractions.extraction_text: As regulations govern only actions within certain defined limits and thereby justify all similar actions that lie outside those limits, I request that my offence is changed from 'unsecured trash' to 'secured trash barring the possibility of bears formulating a strategy in which to take advantage of the thirty seconds between deposit and collection.'

attributes.mechanism: Applies a highly formal legalistic argument to redefine a simple municipal offence, framing a trivial trash issue as a complex legal and strategic problem involving wildlife conspiracy.

extractions.extraction_text: Hello, my name is Simon and these are my good ideas for websites that would definitely make lots of money. Do not copy these ideas because they are mine.

attributes.mechanism: Adopts Simon's voice with complete fidelity to his self-importance and paranoia, letting the character's own framing signal the worthlessness of what follows before a single idea is stated.

extractions.extraction_text: Users could log on anytime to a live webcam and see how their frog is doing and send live requests for me to wave the frog's hand at the camera or bang on the glass if it is sleeping.

attributes.mechanism: Introduces the plausible concept of a pet webcam and escalates into increasingly specific and intrusive interaction requests, presenting each as a natural and acceptable feature of the service.

extractions.extraction_text: Due to my extendable jaw and high acidic saliva levels, I've found that consuming an orange whole and digesting it over the space of many hours requires no effort at all. I can't move much while I'm digesting, but this has not affected my work as I can still move my eyes allowing me to look out of the window and keep my eyes on the petty cash tin.

attributes.mechanism: Employs formal clinical language to describe a grotesque biological impossibility, then links its mundane implications to work productivity, framing the bizarre as a normal aspect of professional life.

extractions.extraction_text: We have been able to reduce the swelling by 85%, bringing his head down to the size of a small family car or large hatchback, similar to the Renault my husband recently bought me.

attributes.mechanism: Presents a medical success story then immediately reveals the 'improved' condition is still grotesquely impossible, compounding the absurdity with a casual domestic comparison that normalises the monstrous.

extractions.extraction_text: Physicists have expressed relief at the news as it was widely considered that Tom's head, if allowed to expand further, could develop its own gravitational field affecting planetary rotation.

attributes.mechanism: Takes the absurd premise of a swelling head to its most extreme scientific conclusion, applying the language and authority of physics to an impossible personal medical condition.
